instruction: How does the symmetry of $ f(x)=\frac{p(x)}{q(x)}$ depend on the symmetry of $p(x)$ and $q(x)$? Select all true statements. There may be more than one correct answer. [ANS] A. If $p(x)$ is even and $q(x)$ is odd, then $f(x)$ is even.  B. If $p(x)$ and $q(x)$ are both even, then $f(x)$ is even.  C. If $p(x)$ is even and $q(x)$ is odd, then $f(x)$ is odd.  D. If $p(x)$ or $q(x)$ is neither even nor odd, then $f(x)$ is neither even nor odd.  E. If $p(x)$ and $q(x)$ are both odd, then $f(x)$ is even.  F. If $p(x)$ is odd and $q(x)$ is even, then $f(x)$ is even.  G. If $p(x)$ is odd and $q(x)$ is even, then $f(x)$ is odd.  H. If $p(x)$ and $q(x)$ are both odd, then $f(x)$ is odd.
